platform.default_exception_rule: Only allow in-place mutation for emergency security fixes or narrow reversible changes, and record the exception in the live-apply receipt plus workstream notes.
notes: ADR 0191 governs active production guests whose hosted services are stateful or edge-facing at redundancy tier R1 or higher.
The inventory guest definition remains the source of truth for VM sizing, template keys, and network identity. This catalog stores only the replacement policy.
The current single-host platform still requires serialized replacement and a bounded rollback window; immutable replacement changes the default delivery path, not the current failure-domain count.

backup.cutover_method: Restore a replacement backup guest from the verified backup seed, validate datastore mounts and Proxmox backup targets privately, then switch scheduled backup jobs to the replacement guest.
backup.rollback_method: Keep the previous backup guest and datastore metadata intact until the replacement completes a full backup cycle and the restore probes stay green.

docker-build.cutover_method: Provision a replacement build guest from the managed Docker-host template, rehydrate the repo workspace and check-runner prerequisites, validate remote builds privately, then move the operator jump target and build workflows to the replacement guest.
docker-build.rollback_method: Keep the previous build guest available so the jump path and build workflows can be pointed back immediately if repo sync, runner bootstrap, or build checks regress.

docker-runtime.cutover_method: Provision a replacement runtime guest from the managed Docker-host template, replay the targeted service converges, validate private control-plane paths first, then repoint shared edge publication and host-side proxies to the replacement guest service by service.
docker-runtime.rollback_method: Retain the previous runtime guest for the rollback window so public edge publication, tailnet proxies, and runtime workloads can be switched back if the replacement misses smoke checks.

monitoring.cutover_method: Provision a replacement monitoring guest, replay the monitoring stack privately, validate Grafana, Prometheus, and exporter health before cutover, then move the published Grafana edge and monitoring writers to the replacement guest.
monitoring.rollback_method: Keep the previous monitoring guest ready for rollback until dashboards, alert evaluations, and guest-writer ingestion stay healthy through the replacement window.

nginx.cutover_method: Provision a replacement edge guest from the managed Debian template, validate the rendered NGINX edge config and published static content while it remains outside public ingress, then switch the host-level TCP 80/443 forwards to the replacement guest.
nginx.rollback_method: Keep the previous edge guest intact so the host-level public forwards can be switched back immediately if TLS, auth, or published-host smoke tests fail.

postgres.cutover_method: Use the warm replica as the live validation and promotion target, perform a controlled Patroni switchover during cutover, then rebuild the previous primary as the next standby.
postgres.rollback_method: Preserve the previous primary long enough to rejoin it as the standby or re-promote it if the new primary fails verification after switchover.
